blocking_gaps: L4: add schema_test.go asserting Schema() property set == config.ApplyMap keys (1:1, no drift) — rubric flags this explicitly; only html/json have it today.

L4: extend the round-trip edge-case matrix beyond UTF-8/LF — add fixtures/tests for CRLF line endings, a UTF-8 BOM, UTF-16 / non-UTF-8 declared encoding, and an all-Unicode value, asserting byte-faithful round-trip. All current fixtures are UTF-8/LF only.
formats.top_risk: Encoding/line-ending blind spot: the writer round-trips by splicing original bytes (good), but resolveValue/renderRunsToXML produce UTF-8 entity-encoded inner content. No test covers a non-UTF-8-declared file, a UTF-8 BOM, or CRLF; the reader stores raw bytes via unsafe.String and never re-encodes, so a translated value in a UTF-16 doc could mix encodings undetected — untested.

blocking_gaps: schema_test.go: add a test asserting Schema() property set == Config ApplyMap accepted keys (extractComments, protectPlaceholders) so schema and config can't drift — required for L4.

Edge-case encoding matrix: TestUTF16RoundTrip only covers UTF-16LE+BOM; the writer/encoding.go claim UTF-16BE support but no test asserts a UTF-16BE byte-faithful round-trip. Add UTF-16BE (and a no-BOM UTF-8) round-trip case for the L4 encoding matrix.
Line-ending / CRLF edge case: no test proves a CRLF-delimited .strings table round-trips byte-exact; add one to the L4 edge-case matrix.
formats.top_risk: UTF-16BE is a documented capability (encoding.go / writer comment / nativedocs) but is NOT covered by any round-trip assertion — only UTF-16LE is tested (roundtrip_test.go:314 TestUTF16RoundTrip). A BE encode/decode regression would pass CI silently.

blocking_gaps: schema_test asserting schema==config (L4): no test pins Config.Schema() property/key set to the ApplyMap-accepted keys (descriptionNotes), so schema/config drift would go uncaught.

Edge-case round-trip matrix (L4): add fixtures exercising the scanner's BOM and CRLF code paths (skipWhitespace handles 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF and \r\n) plus surrogate-pair \uXXXX escapes and mixed encodings; currently no test feeds a BOM/CRLF/astral-escape file through byte-faithful round-trip.

From-scratch writer fidelity test or hardening (L4): buildCanonical drops all @<id> attributes except description and re-sorts keys alphabetically — no test asserts/limits this loss, and it is a silent non-byte-faithful path for synthetic pipelines.
formats.top_risk: Lossy from-scratch path: writer.writeFromScratch -> buildCanonical (encode.go) discards every @<id> attribute except description and alphabetically re-sorts keys (loses source order). A synthetic pipeline that produces ARB blocks without an arb.original Layer property will emit a structurally-degraded file. Documented as 'out of scope' but untested and silent.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go: feed truncated/broken-quote/binary-garbage and header-only input to Read and assert require.NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel (dimension 6, high weight — the single biggest L4 blocker).

Add a test exercising the useCodeFinder/codeFinderRules path: configure CodeFinderRules, read a cell, assert applyCodeFinder produced Ph runs (SourceRuns with PlaceholderRun) with verbatim Data and that they round-trip (closes the Reader inline-span gap).
Add useCodeFinder + codeFinderRules to spec.yaml as documented ConfigKeys so spec keys are 1:1 with ApplyMap (dimension 4).
formats.top_risk: Embedded newline inside a quoted field is NOT byte-stable on round-trip — test2cols.csv is explicitly excluded from the upstream corpus test (upstream_test.go L122-123) for this reason, and TestRoundTrip_EmbeddedNewlines exists but splitRawLines tracks quotes only via a bare '"' toggle that LazyQuotes-recovered/malformed quoting can desync, so the skeleton may mis-segment lines on real-world dirty CSV.

blocking_gaps: L4: add an edge-case round-trip matrix — encodings (non-UTF-8 / BOM), CRLF vs LF line endings, and unicode/escape edge cases — currently round-trip fixtures are all UTF-8/LF
L4: add a schema_test that asserts the schema properties match the Config fields (schema==config) so the single extractDescriptions toggle cannot drift from schema.go
L4: assert Open(nil-doc) rejection in a test (the code rejects it at reader.go:112 but no test exercises the nil path)
formats.top_risk: Fidelity depends entirely on the delegated json.Writer's original-bytes-retokenize replay (json.original/json.keypath); a regression in core/formats/json could silently weaken designtokens round-trip — the corpus is only a single upstream file (style-dictionary-demo), so breadth of real-world DTCG variety is thin (one source repo).

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (HIGH): feed truncated/broken Doxygen source (e.g. unterminated /** with no */, lone /*! at EOF, binary garbage, CRLF-mangled) and assert require.NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error or graceful empty extraction on the channel — required for L2 and currently absent.

Harden Detection (MEDIUM): add a Sniff to the doxygen FormatSignature (or drop the bare generic .c/.cpp/.h/.java/.m/.py extensions) so a plain source file with no /// or /** doc comment is not claimed by doxygen over a more generic handler.
formats.top_risk: No malformed/robustness test: reader.go reads the whole file with io.ReadAll then line-splits and indexes lines[i]; an adversarial or truncated comment (unterminated /** ... with no closing */ at EOF, lone /*!< etc.) is never exercised for NotPanics, so a slice/index or parser edge-panic could ship undetected.

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/dtd/malformed_test.go: feed truncated/broken DTD (unclosed <!ENTITY ..., unclosed <!--, unclosed <?, garbage bytes, invalid UTF-8) and assert NotPanics + that the reader either emits a clean PartResult.Error or terminates cleanly; explicitly assert nil-doc Open rejection. (high weight; this is the single strictest unmet L2 item)
Add core/formats/dtd/schema.go implementing a SchemaProvider for useCodeFinder/codeFinderRules and register via registerSchemaAndDecoder so the config surface is exposed to CLI/UI/reference. (high)

Fix spec.yaml drift: add a `keys:` section mapping useCodeFinder + codeFinderRules 1:1 to ApplyMap with okapi_param (USECODEFINDER, codeFinderRules), and delete the stale header comment claiming ApplyMap rejects all input / has no configurable knobs. (high)
formats.top_risk: Malformed input is silently swallowed, not surfaced: an unclosed <!ENTITY, unclosed comment, unclosed <!...> declaration, or unclosed <?...?> hits a bare `break` in readContent (reader.go lines 176, 248, 266) and the loop just stops — no PartResult.Error is emitted, so trailing content is silently dropped with no signal. There is zero test coverage proving this is panic-safe or that callers can detect the truncation.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (HIGH): truncated/corrupt ZIP, missing META-INF/container.xml, malformed OPF, empty/non-zip bytes — assert require.NotPanics AND a clean PartResult.Error on the channel; also assert Open(nil) (already covered) — required for L2.

Add schema.go (HIGH): even a parameterless schema for CLI/UI/reference; the rubric requires schema.go present for L2 (Config has no params so a schema_test asserting schema==config is trivial and would also satisfy part of L4).
formats.top_risk: No byte-fidelity round-trip test against the ORIGINAL archive. sourcepath_test.go (TestSourcePath_ByteEqualToOriginalContent) only proves the SourcePath writer and the OriginalContent writer agree byte-for-byte with EACH OTHER — it does NOT prove either equals the input EPUB. TestRoundTrip/TestSkeletonRoundTrip assert text Contains / block-count / re-read text equality (semantic), so XHTML re-serialization drift (attribute ordering, whitespace, self-closing form via Go's xml encoder in replaceXHTMLText) is undetected.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go: feed truncated/garbage/lone-CR/invalid-UTF8 input and assert NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error (or graceful empty stream) on the channel — this is the L2 robustness requirement and the single item gating the jump from L1 to its earned L3.

Add schema.go (CLI/UI/reference) + schema_test asserting schema==config; the nativedocs sidecar currently admits 'no JSON schema is published', leaving the high-weight Config+Schema dimension only half-met.
formats.top_risk: No malformed/truncated-input test at all: a lone '\r', invalid UTF-8, or io.ReadAll error path (reader.go:110-114) is unproven against panic. The rune-slicing in extractColumn (reader.go:321-329) and skeleton gap logic (reader.go:169-179) are untested on degenerate lines beyond the happy short-line case.

blocking_gaps: Add a dedicated malformed_test.go asserting NotPanics + clean behavior on truncated/binary-garbage/empty input (and confirm the PartResult.Error path is reachable), to satisfy L4 robustness across an edge-case matrix.

Make ApplyMap reject unknown keys (replace both 'default: // ignore' branches with an error, or switch to ApplyMapViaJSON with DisallowUnknownFields) — currently a typo'd config key is silently dropped, which is the one concrete L4-blocking correctness hole.

Formalize the encoding/line-ending/unicode edge-case matrix as explicit byte-faithful roundtrip assertions (BOM, CRLF, UTF-16) — currently BOM/encoding tests only assert content extraction, not byte-exact retarget.
formats.top_risk: ApplyMap silently ignores unknown keys (config.go:111, applyParserSettings:127 'default: // Ignore for forward compatibility') — a mistyped preset key or a removed param is dropped with no error, so the schema_test's schema==ApplyMap guarantee does not extend to runtime rejection of bad input.

blocking_gaps: schema_test asserting schema.go Properties == config.go ApplyMap keys (catch drift) — required for L4 'schema_test asserts schema==config'
Byte-faithful edge-case matrix: non-UTF-8 encodings, BOM, CRLF line endings, and unicode-escape preservation exercised at the i18next layer (currently only default UTF-8 fixtures)

Round-trip / recovery assertion for the opt-in _html HTML-subfilter branch (currently the only non-byte-faithful, documented-normalization path and it has no fidelity guarantee, only TestHTMLSubfilterOptIn checks a child layer appears)
formats.top_risk: schema.go and config.go list the 3 keys independently with no test asserting they stay in sync — silent drift possible (e.g. adding a config key without a schema property, or vice versa).

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (HIGH): feed truncated/broken/non-XML and nil-reader input; assert a clean PartResult.Error on the channel under NotPanics. Currently the reader breaks its decode loop on any token error and emits NO error part, so malformed input silently yields partial output — both undetected and untested. Required for L2.

Add schema.go (HIGH): make Config implement SchemaProvider so the format exposes a JSON schema for CLI/UI/reference (the nativedocs sidecar explicitly notes its absence); ideally back it with a schema_test asserting schema==config. Required for the L2 'schema.go present' criterion.
formats.top_risk:
Inline codes are LOST, not preserved (dimension 1): the reader concatenates Content text across CharacterStyleRange runs and ignores
(soft-break path) and footnote/inline anchors, producing a single plain-text Block with no Ph/Pc runs. Byte-exactness survives ONLY because the unchanged spans are copied verbatim from the skeleton; the moment a translated Block re-spans multiple original elements, the writer's replaceSequential collapses them into one replacement span (writer.go 305-347), which can shift inline formatting boundaries. A translation tool sees flattened text and cannot honor original code placement.

blocking_gaps: schema.go: add a SchemaProvider implementation (config.go Schema()) exposing the 6 boolean keys for CLI/UI/reference + a schema_test asserting schema==config — required L2 high-weight item, currently absent (nativedocs sidecar explicitly notes no JSON Schema).

malformed_test.go: add tests feeding a truncated ZIP byte-prefix and a corrupt/unbalanced story XML, asserting NotPanics AND a clean PartResult.Error on the channel — required L2 high-weight item; current coverage only handles non-ZIP garbage and nil doc, not truncation/recoverable corruption.
formats.top_risk: spec<->config key drift: spec.yaml declares container keys extraction/formatting/inlineCodes that ApplyMap would REJECT as unknown, and OMITS extractHyperlinkTextSourcesInline which ApplyMap accepts. Keys are not 1:1; a consumer feeding the spec's container keys through ApplyMap would get an error.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go: feed a matrix of broken/truncated inputs (unterminated string, unterminated object/array, lone surrogate, garbage bytes, deeply-truncated nesting) and assert assert.NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel for each — current invalid-JSON tests only check 'an error appeared', no NotPanics, no truncation matrix (L2 gate).

Reconcile the spec.yaml default drift: spec.yaml documents escapeForwardSlashes and useLeadingSlashOnKeyPath as 'Default off/false', but Config.Reset() and schema.go both set them true (Okapi escapeForwardSlashes defaults true). Fix spec.yaml (or the code) so all three agree (L2 spec-quality).
Run the head-to-head parity suite (cli/parity/formats/json_spec_test.go) against okapi-bridge and confirm zero unattributed/pure-default-diff xfails; add bridge_config where param shapes differ (L3).
formats.top_risk: Default drift across three sources: Config.Reset() sets EscapeForwardSlashes=true & UseLeadingSlashOnKeyPath=true; schema.go declares both Default:true; spec.yaml documents both as default false ('Default off'). schema_test.go only checks key-set parity, NOT default equality, so the drift is uncaught. A consumer reading spec.yaml will get the opposite escaping/slash behavior than the engine produces.

blocking_gaps: Add a malformed_test (high weight, L2): feed truncated/unclosed/garbage markdown (e.g. unterminated front matter, mid-byte UTF-8, very large nesting) and assert require.NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error or graceful Block emission on the channel — currently zero NotPanics in the package.

Add a corpus/upstream test (high weight, L3): wire at least one real-world markdown file via spec.yaml input_file: okapi:... or a corpus_test reading upstream fixtures, instead of only authored inline snippets.
formats.top_risk: No malformed-input coverage: goldmark + the hand-rolled handleFrontMatter byte scanner (reader.go:225-279, manual index arithmetic on content[i:i+3]) and the trailSpaceTrimmer are untested against truncated/adversarial input — a panic-on-malformed regression would not be caught.

blocking_gaps: schema_test.go asserting schema.go output matches config.go fields/types/defaults (the one missing L4 harvest item; only html/json have this today)

Optional: an explicit Open(nil)/Read robustness unit test for the nil-doc path (currently only the message string exists in code; malformed_test covers content-level robustness but not the nil-doc rejection assertion)
formats.top_risk: v1 scope limitation (not a bug, but a fidelity ceiling): JSX children prose, JSX attribute string values, ESM, expressions, AND GFM tables are ALL preserved opaque and NEVER translated. Real docs carry user-facing copy inside <Callout>…</Callout> children and table cells; that text is silently untranslatable. The corpus 'translated MDX compiles' acceptance only proves prose-only translation is valid MDX, not that all translatable content is reached.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go: feed truncated/broken patterns and assert NotPanics + a clean error path (high-weight, hard L2 requirement, currently entirely absent)

Move parse errors off Open() and onto the channel as PartResult.Error per the Reader dimension contract; broken input currently fails in Open() not as a channel Error (taints the critical Reader dimension and the malformed-robustness shape)

Make the non-skeleton writer path faithful: render via RenderRunsWithData (or equivalent) so Ph placeholder runs ({0}/{name}/#) are not dropped when no skeleton store is attached — currently getBlockText()->SourceText() loses them
formats.top_risk: Parse errors are returned from Open() (reader.go:93-95, :151-153) instead of emitted as PartResult.Error on the channel — contradicts the Reader dimension's stated contract; consumers that only inspect channel results would never see the error during Read.

blocking_gaps: Add a malformed_test.go (HIGH): feed truncated/garbage/unbalanced-brace MIF and assert require.NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel (currently only nil-doc Open and clean inputs are covered) — required L2 gate.

Add schema.go implementing SchemaProvider (HIGH): the 12 ApplyMap keys are documented in spec.yaml + nativedocs but Config exposes no JSON Schema for CLI/UI/reference; nativedocs/formats/mif.yaml explicitly notes the absence — required L2 'schema.go present' gate.
Add a schema_test (MEDIUM, for L4 later) asserting schema == config keys once schema.go exists, so the two cannot drift.
formats.top_risk: tab_between_strings expected_fail attribution is imprecise: the reason blames the bridge (#509), but the has_block_with_text:"\t" assertion ALSO fails the native side in the parity run (native log: 'has_block_with_text: "\t" not found'). The native reader DOES decode the tab as a Ph (proven by TestReadMIFSpecialCharacters), but the spec assertion checks text-only SourceText, so it can never see Ph data — the divergence is partly an assertion-shape mismatch, not a pure engine divergence. Not a clean bridge-only xfail.

blocking_gaps: Config.ApplyMap (core/formats/mo/config.go) must REJECT unknown keys — replace the no-op `for key := range values { _ = key }` loop with a default branch that returns an error on any unrecognized key (or switch to ApplyMapViaJSON with DisallowUnknownFields). This is the L1 config-validation requirement and the single criterion blocking L1.
Add okapi_skip_test.go documenting WHY there is no Okapi counterpart (gettext MO is not an Okapi filter) — the required marker for a harvest format and a prerequisite the rubric ties to L2.

Add a malformed/robustness test asserting NotPanics + clean error on degenerate inputs (nil Output already guarded; cover empty-source blocks, nil Properties, duplicate (ctxt,msgid) keys, empty stream) — required for L2.
formats.top_risk: Config silently swallows unknown keys: a typo'd writer option in a .kapi recipe (e.g. `endianness: big`) is accepted and ignored, with no error — the only true correctness defect found.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (the named L2 requirement, weight high): feed truncated/binary/invalid-UTF-8 input through reader and assert assert.NotPanics + a clean result (no panic, no error since the format tolerates all bytes; or PartResult.Error if a decode failure mode is introduced). This is the single gap holding the format below L2 despite spec/parity/schema-doc all being in place.

Add a schema.go (or have Config implement format.SchemaProvider) so the format surfaces a JSON Schema for CLI/UI/reference — currently intentionally absent and documented, but a real Config+Schema dimension shortfall for the high-weight tier.
formats.top_risk: No malformed/robustness test at all: nothing asserts NotPanics on truncated/binary/invalid-UTF-8 input. scanRawLines does io.ReadAll(string(data)) and string indexing into regex match offsets (parseCodes/applyCodeFinder slice text[loc[0]:loc[1]]) — on adversarial multibyte or pathological inline-markup input a panic or silent mis-slice is unverified.

blocking_gaps: Add schema.go implementing SchemaProvider for the two config keys (CLI/UI/reference) plus a schema_test — required for L2; peer ZIP format epub already has it.

Add malformed_test.go that feeds truncated/corrupt ODF (bad ZIP, and crucially malformed inner content.xml) and asserts NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error — required for L2. Also fix reader.go:421-423 to emit the d.Token() error on the channel instead of silently break-ing.

Give spec.yaml at least one synthetic (non-okapi:) example so TestSpec actually executes and is provably green in a checkout without okapi-testdata; today the entire spec_test + all corpus/parity tests SKIP locally (17 of 36 tests skip).
formats.top_risk: Malformed inner XML is silently swallowed: parseODFContent breaks on ANY d.Token() error (reader.go:421-423) without emitting PartResult.Error, so a truncated/corrupt content.xml yields silent partial extraction rather than a clean error — and there is no malformed_test to catch this.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go: feed truncated ZIP, garbage bytes, and corrupt-but-recoverable OOXML; assert NotPanics + a single clean PartResult.Error on the channel (high weight; this is the only HIGH-weight L2/L3 gap still open — the absence is a real robustness gap per the rubric).
Add a schema_test asserting schema.go properties == config.go ApplyMap keys (L4 requirement; today only html/json have schema_test).

Add an explicit edge-case round-trip matrix test (encodings, CRLF/LF line endings, unicode/CJK, BOM, line-separator U+2028 replacement path) with byte-faithful assertions, beyond the current per-corpus untouched-part check.
formats.top_risk: No malformed/panic-safety test: truncated or adversarial ZIP/OOXML input is unverified for NotPanics. zip.NewReader and the XML decoders look safe by inspection but a crafted central-directory or deeply nested part could panic without a regression guard.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go: feed a failing/erroring io.Reader (and ideally a non-UTF8 byte stream) and assert NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel — required for L2 robustness.
Add schema.go exposing the format schema for CLI/UI/reference — L2 requires schema.go present (even an empty-params schema, since Config has no knobs).

Make the DEFAULT (no-skeleton) writer path faithful or remove it: it hardcodes '\n\n' (writer.go:140) so it loses CRLF and triple-blank separators; either route round-trip exclusively through the skeleton path or render via RenderRunsWithData with a byte-exact assertion, so there is ONE declared round-trip strategy actually proven.
formats.top_risk: Dual round-trip paths with divergent fidelity: only the skeleton path is byte-exact. Any caller using the writer without a SkeletonStore gets a lossy rebuild (every separator collapses to '\n\n', CRLF and 3+ blank-line runs are lost). The no-skeleton TestRoundTrip masks this by asserting only Contains(...), so the regression net does NOT protect default-path fidelity.

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/pdf/malformed_test.go: feed truncated/garbage PDFs (no %%EOF, 'stream' with no 'endstream', unbalanced '(' literal, raw non-PDF bytes) through Open+Read and assert NotPanics + that read errors arrive as PartResult.Error on the channel (or that no block is produced) — the one high-weight item blocking L2/L3 (code is empirically panic-free but untested).
Add an invariants/acceptance test pinning the read-only contract (HasWriter false at the format level is in register_test, but a per-format invariants_test would harden the L2 set).
Optional: add a reference-data sidecar entry for pdf to match the convention used by other formats (nativedocs sidecar already exists).
formats.top_risk: No malformed_test: robustness against truncated/corrupt PDFs is unverified in CI. extractStreams does header[headerStart-lookback:headerStart] slicing and unbounded bytes.Index scans on attacker-controlled offsets; a future refactor could introduce a slice-bounds panic with no regression test to catch it (I confirmed current code does not panic, but nothing locks that in).

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (high): feed truncated/garbage PHP (unterminated single/double quote, unterminated heredoc, lone <?php, binary noise) and assert NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel; assert Open(nil doc) rejected. Today the reader CANNOT surface parse errors at all — likely requires reader changes to emit PartResult.Error, not just a test.

Decide intended robustness contract: tolerant-best-effort (then assert deterministic non-panicking degradation) vs error-surfacing (then make the lexer detect+report malformations). Currently undefined.
These two satisfy the L2 'malformed_test asserts clean errors no panic' requirement; spec.yaml + spec_test + schema.go are already in place, so malformed is the sole L2 blocker.
formats.top_risk: Reader never emits PartResult.Error (no 'Error:' anywhere in reader.go). Unterminated single/double-quoted strings and heredocs silently produce a best-effort token (parseDoubleQuoted/parseHeredoc return what they have up to EOF). Malformed input is wholly untested — a panic or silent data loss on adversarial input would not be caught.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (HIGH, robustness): feed broken/truncated input and an erroring Reader; assert NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error arrives on the channel (exercises reader.go:98 io.ReadAll error and reader.go:103 ToUTF8 transcode-failure paths, currently untested). This is the single item separating L1 from L2.
Optional for solidifying L2: add an invariants_test or dedicated corpus_test (MEDIUM) — current real-file coverage is only synthetic testdata via TestRoundTrip_TestFiles + spec input_file.
formats.top_risk: Non-skeleton writer path drops CRLF: writeBlock/writeData use fmt.Fprintln (writer.go:133,148) which always emits LF, so a roundtrip WITHOUT a skeleton store (the default registry-constructed writer when no skeleton is wired) silently normalizes CRLF/bare-CR to LF and collapses multi-blank-line spacing. Byte-fidelity is only guaranteed on the skeleton path; the fallback path is lossy and has no test asserting its (non-)fidelity.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go: feed truncated/garbage/orphan-msgstr/binary input and assert NotPanics + (where applicable) a clean PartResult.Error on the channel; this is the L2 robustness requirement currently blocking L3 (high weight).

Add a corpus_test/upstream_test on real upstream .po files OR add input_file: okapi:... examples to spec.yaml — currently only one synthetic testdata/simple.po and one okapi-fixture origin (high weight, L3 requirement).

Once malformed + corpus land, the format is parity-wired and ready for L3; for L4 add a schema_test (schema==config), an explicit encoding/line-ending edge-case matrix test, and confirm zero native-bug xfails in the live parity dashboard.
formats.top_risk: Reader is an infallible best-effort parser: no input ever produces a PartResult.Error and malformed PO (unterminated quotes, stray msgstr[, orphan msgstr, binary bytes) is silently swallowed into whatever blocks parse — corruption could pass undetected with no diagnostic. Not covered by any committed test.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (HIGH): feed truncated input, a dangling trailing backslash continuation at EOF, a lone '\u' / short-hex escape, and an empty/whitespace-only file; assert assert.NotPanics and a clean channel (LayerStart/LayerEnd emitted, no Error). This is the single strictest unmet L2 criterion — everything else for L2 (spec.yaml + green spec_test, schema.go) is already present.

Add an invariants/acceptance angle (MEDIUM, supports L2 for a parity-mapped format): there is no invariants_test; the double-extraction stability test lives in okapi_stubs_test.go (TestRoundTrip_DoubleExtraction) which covers re-extraction equality but not structural invariants per se.
formats.top_risk: No malformed/robustness test at all: parseHexRune failure, EOF-mid-continuation (readLogicalLines' trailing inContinuation branch), and lone trailing backslash are all coded but untested — a regression there would pass CI silently.

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/regex/malformed_test.go: feed an uncompilable pattern and assert the reader emits a clean PartResult.Error on the channel with NotPanics; feed truncated/garbage input and assert no panic and a usable error/Data stream (HIGH weight — currently the sole hard miss vs L4-readiness, and the error path is wholly untested).

Add an edge-case round-trip matrix (encodings, CRLF vs LF line endings, multi-byte UTF-8 in captured values, BOM, malformed-but-recoverable input) asserting byte-exact reconstruction — required for L4 'rock-solid'.
Add a real-world / upstream corpus test (corpus_test or spec.yaml input_file: okapi:...) beyond the small authored snippets and synthetic fixtures.
formats.top_risk: No robustness test at all: the invalid-regex-compile and io.ReadAll error branches (reader.go ln 124-128, 103-107) emit on the channel but are never exercised — a regression that turned an emitError into a panic or an Open-return-error would pass CI silently.

blocking_gaps: L4: add a dedicated edge-case-matrix round-trip test (encodings incl. non-UTF8/declared charsets, CRLF vs LF line endings, full Unicode incl. surrogate-pair/astral chars, malformed-but-recoverable variants) beyond the current 3 fixtures + 4-file corpus
L4: add schema_test.go asserting schema.go Properties == config.go ApplyMap keys 1:1 (currently schema.go has no test, so schema/config drift is uncaught)
L4: make the resgen/xmllint acceptance checks a standing guarantee rather than build-tagged + exec.LookPath-skipped (today they are inert in the normal/CI pass)
formats.top_risk: buildValueRuns placeholder lifting uses strings.IndexByte for the closing '}' and assumes .NET placeholders never nest; a value containing a literal '{' followed later by an unrelated '}' (not a real composite-format token) would be mis-lifted into a Ph run. SourceText() then drops it (TestPlaceholderProtection shows 'You have  items' with the {0} elided), so a tool reading SourceText loses content — round-trip is still byte-safe because Ph.Data is verbatim, but the segmentation is heuristic, not spec-grounded against .NET's actual FormatString grammar.

blocking_gaps: Add a malformed_test.go (HIGH): feed truncated/unbalanced RTF (dangling braces, truncated \uN/\'HH, unterminated groups, nested fallback groups) and assert require.NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel; assert Open(nil) rejection already covered. Absence currently blocks L2.

Make the native spec_test actually verify the native side (HIGH/correctness): every spec.yaml example is tagged expected_fail, which in core/format/spec/runner.go demotes native EvalAssertions failures from t.Errorf to t.Logf (lines 88-96). The spec's assertions describe NATIVE behavior yet are silently unverified. Either (a) split the spec so native examples are NOT expected_fail (and only the bridge cross-check carries the xfail), or (b) introduce a divergence_kind that keeps native assertions hard while marking only bridge divergence soft.

Preserve inline codes as runs (HIGH for true L1 fidelity): reader flattens \b/\b0 and other inline formatting out of block text instead of emitting Ph/PcOpen/PcClose runs with verbatim Data; writer should then render via RenderRunsWithData. Until then non-trivial RTF is not run-faithful and the byte-exact skeleton path only holds for code-free blocks.
formats.top_risk: Spec_test is effectively a no-op verifier for native correctness: because all 6 features' examples carry expected_fail, core/format/spec/runner.go logs (never fails) native assertion mismatches. The spec's native block_text/block_count assertions could silently rot and the always-on test would stay green. Real native verification lives ONLY in hand-written reader_test.go.

blocking_gaps: L2: add schema.go + malformed_test.go (has spec.yaml+spec_test+parity)
formats.top_risk: Internal line-splicing helper; no schema.go, no malformed_test; gap-analysis agent did not return structured output.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go: feed truncated/garbage cues (missing timecode, half cue at EOF, non-numeric sequence) and assert require.NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel — and add the Error-on-channel path to the reader (currently no parse error is ever emitted).

Model inline SubRip styling tags (…, , {\an8}) as Ph/PcOpen/PcClose runs with verbatim Data instead of dropping them into plain block text, matching Okapi srt.fprm's codeFinder; add a reader_test assertion on SourceRuns()/code preservation.
Add an invariants_test (or strengthen corpus_test) over a real .srt corpus rather than the single authored simple.srt — Okapi's srt.fprm ships a realistic sample to seed it.
formats.top_risk: Inline codes are silently flattened: Italic survives ONLY because the skeleton path replays original bytes verbatim; the moment a tool edits/translates such a block, the tags live inside the translated string with no code protection, so a translator/MT can corrupt or drop them — and TestSkeletonStore_ByteExact_FormattingTags gives false confidence because it never translates the tagged cue.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go: feed truncated/broken TeX (unterminated \begin, runaway brace, invalid UTF-8, empty input) and assert NotPanics + that any error surfaces as a clean PartResult.Error on the channel; also assert Open(ctx, nil) is rejected (already true). This is the single highest-weight blocker to L2.
Add schema.go (even an empty/no-param schema) so CLI/UI/reference can introspect the format; rubric requires schema.go present for L2.
Add an invariants_test or corpus_test that exercises real-world / upstream .tex files (e.g. spec.yaml input_file: okapi:... or a corpus harness) rather than only synthetic snippets.
formats.top_risk: Round-trip byte-fidelity leans on block.Properties["tex.rawSource"] plus extractLeadingWhitespace/extractTrailingWhitespace heuristics in writer.go (blockTextForSkeleton); the trailing-whitespace restore is explicitly a 1-byte-drift workaround for paragraphs adjacent to TeX commands. Edge cases (mixed CRLF, multiple trailing blank lines, tabs) are untested and could drift.

blocking_gaps: Malformed/robustness (high): convert the non-asserting TestInvalidXml/TestOpenInvalidContent/TestOpenEmptyContent into real assertions (assert.NotPanics around Read + assert a clean PartResult.Error or a well-formed empty result), or add a dedicated malformed_test.go covering truncated/garbled/binary input like arb/resx/xcstrings.

spec.yaml keys 1:1 with ApplyMap (high): either implement the documented-but-unhonored keys (segType, consolidateDpSkeleton, propValueSep, inlineCodes parent map) in ApplyMap, or remove them from the config: block so the spec keys map exactly to native ApplyMap params + okapi_param. The rubric requires keys 1:1.

Real-world corpus (medium, needed for L3): add an upstream_test/corpus_test or a spec example with input_file: okapi:... pointing at real Trados/Okapi TMX fixtures (UTF-16+BOM, large multilingual TMs) rather than the single 643-byte synthetic simple.tmx.
formats.top_risk: Robustness tests are theatrical: TestInvalidXml, TestOpenInvalidContent, TestOpenEmptyContent read the channel then discard the error ('_ = err') with no assert.NotPanics and no Error assertion — malformed input could start panicking or silently dropping content and these tests would still pass. This is the rubric's exact 'absence is a real gap' case.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go asserting clean Error on the channel + NotPanics for truncated input, binary/non-UTF8 bytes, a data row with a crumb missing the okpCtx:tu= prefix, and a header-only-but-malformed-cells file (high-weight L2 robustness requirement; today only TestInvalidSignature + nil-doc exist, no NotPanics)
Add schema.go publishing the allowSegments parameter for CLI/UI/reference (high-weight L2 Config+Schema requirement; currently absent)

Strengthen the skeleton/round-trip test to actually prove fidelity: assert full-document semantic equality (or a documented+justified normalization such as always-quoted-cells / re-rendered header), not just assert.Contains substrings — and decide whether byte-exact replay of the verbatim header the reader buffers is the contract or document why it is re-rendered
formats.top_risk: Round-trip fidelity is unproven: skeleton_test.go and TestRoundTripWithTargetLocale use only assert.Contains, never full-output or byte equality. The writer re-renders the header from writer locale (writer.go lines 150-151,157) instead of replaying the verbatim header bytes the reader stored (reader.go line 152), and always emits quoted cells even when input was unquoted — so an unquoted-input document does NOT round-trip byte-exact, and no test would catch a regression there.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (HIGH): feed truncated/broken-XML and a non-TS payload; assert reader.Read emits a PartResult.Error on the channel under assert.NotPanics, and that Open rejects nil (extend beyond the current nil-only test). Required for L2.
Add schema.go (HIGH): a CLI/UI/reference JSON schema for the format. Required for L2 ('schema.go present'). Note html/json are the cited exemplars.

Reconcile Config keys with spec.yaml (HIGH): either implement decodeByteValues / escapeGT / inlineCodes in Config.ApplyMap so keys are 1:1, or formally mark them as parity-only in a schema_test/spec note that the drift detector accepts — today ApplyMap rejects every documented key, so keys are not 1:1.
formats.top_risk: Config/spec drift: spec.yaml advertises 3 real Okapi params (decodeByteValues, escapeGT, inlineCodes) that the native reader silently ignores — a user setting decodeByteValues=true gets an 'unknown parameter' error from ApplyMap, while the spec implies the knob exists. The behavior is documented but is a real UX/parity hazard.

blocking_gaps:
Add malformed_test.go: feed truncated/broken XML and a malformed-but-present

, assert require.NotPanics and that a PartResult.Error reaches the channel (today parseCaptions/findPTextRanges silently `break` on decoder errors -> silent partial extraction, no surfaced error). This is the strictest unmet L2 item.

Preserve inline codes per the rubric's L1/Reader contract OR formally bless the unwrap-to-text behavior: /
should become Ph/PcOpen/PcClose runs with verbatim Data so a

containing inline markup round-trips byte-exact. Today spans/br are flattened, so the byte-exact fidelity claim does not cover captions with inline markup.

Add an invariants/corpus assertion exercising a real upstream TTML file (or a span-inside-

byte-exact skeleton case) — current byte-exact skeleton_test cases all use

with plain text only, so the strongest fidelity scenario (markup inside a caption) is untested.
formats.top_risk:
Byte-exact round-trip is only proven for

elements whose inner content is plain text. The skeleton ref for a

writes block.SourceText() (the UNWRAPPED text); a

containing a or
would have that inline markup replaced by flattened text on write, breaking byte-exactness. skeleton_test.go has no span-inside-

case, so this hole is unguarded (TestSkeletonStore_ByteExact_WithStyling only puts tts:* on the

tag itself, not inline children).

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/ttx/malformed_test.go: feed truncated <TRADOStag, broken UTF-16/BOM bytes, and a <Tu> missing </Tuv>; assert NotPanics and that a clean PartResult.Error lands on the channel (and nil-doc already rejected by Open). [high — primary L2 blocker]

Re-author spec.yaml fixtures to the real TTX shape (text directly in <Tuv>, no <Seg> wrapper) so the spec represents the format the reader actually targets and matches the Okapi fixtures. [high — spec fidelity]

Add schema.go implementing SchemaProvider for ttx (segmentMode enum, escapeGT bool) so the Config+Schema dimension is satisfied for CLI/UI/reference and a schema_test can assert schema==config. [high]
formats.top_risk: Hollow parity: 100% of cli/parity ttx examples are expected_fail with the bridge producing 0 blocks — there is no single example where native and okapi-bridge agree. The 'bridge bug #486' attribution masks the real cause (the spec's <Seg> fixtures are not valid TTX), so the parity test proves nothing about native↔Okapi fidelity. Effectively whole-happy-path default-diff xfails.

blocking_gaps: Add schema.go implementing format.SchemaProvider on the txml Config (Schema() returning the allowEmptyOutputTarget + inlineCodes JSON schema) so registerSchemaAndDecoder wires CLI/UI/reference; add schema_test asserting schema keys == ApplyMap keys (high weight, L2 requirement).

Add malformed_test.go: feed truncated/broken TXML (unclosed <translatable>, dangling <ut>, garbage bytes, partial <txml root) and assert require.NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel; assert Open(nil) rejection already covered (high weight, L2 requirement).

Verify (and keep green) the parity head-to-head: run cli/parity/formats/txml_spec_test.go with -tags parity against okapi-bridge 1.47.0 + checked-out okapi-testdata, confirming the 3 native-more-correct divergences are the ONLY ones (needed to actually attain L3, not just be wired for it).
formats.top_risk: No malformed/robustness test at all: a truncated <ut>/<segment>/<translatable> or non-XML bytes is handled only implicitly (decoder.Strict=false + EOF->channel error). Nothing asserts NotPanics or a clean error, so a parser panic or silent partial-block emission on malformed input would go undetected.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go (high): feed truncated/binary/huge-line/invalid-UTF-8 input and assert require.NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel; also assert Open(nil) error path stays clean. This high-weight robustness test is entirely absent.
Add schema.go (high): even though Config has no parameters, L2 requires a schema.go for CLI/UI/reference; pair with a schema_test (or document why an empty schema is correct).

Add okapi_skip_test.go + invariants_test.go + corpus_test.go (high): for a native-only/harvest format the rubric expects an explicit okapi_skip_test attesting the no-bridge-counterpart decision, an invariants test (e.g. read->write byte-exact over a generated matrix), and a corpus test over real verse files.
formats.top_risk: No malformed/robustness test: nothing proves the reader does not panic on binary, invalid-UTF-8, or pathological input. bufio.Scanner has a default 64KB line limit (bufio.MaxScanTokenSize) in the non-skeleton path (readContentSimple), so a single very long line would surface as scanner.Err -> emitted as Error, but this is untested; the skeleton path uses bufio.Reader.ReadString and has no such limit (asymmetry between the two paths is untested).

blocking_gaps: Add schema.go for the package (CLI/UI/reference surface) — required for L2; today only config.go exists.
Add malformed_test.go: feed truncated/broken/non-XML input and assert NotPanics + a single clean PartResult.Error on the channel; also pin Open(nil) rejection there.

Make the spec_test actually enforce the contract: remove the expected_fail suppression on the native side for the four content examples (simple_doc_one_pair, complex_doc_two_pairs, real_world_test01, plain_payload_extracts_once) so block_count/first_block_text/block_texts assertions are enforced (t.Errorf), not logged.
formats.top_risk: SUPPRESSED SPEC CONTRACT: every content-bearing example carries expected_fail. In BOTH runners (core/format/spec/runner.go:88-96 native, cli/parity/spec/runner.go:212-225 parity) an expected_fail tag downgrades ALL assertion failures to t.Logf and returns early. So spec.yaml's block_count/first_block_text/block_texts are NOT enforced by spec_test.go for 4 of 6 examples — they 'pass' only because they are not checked. Native correctness IS independently and rigorously enforced by hand-written reader_test.go, so the format is not actually broken, but the spec is not a binding contract here.

blocking_gaps: spec.yaml config keys must be 1:1 with ApplyMap: either add discardCues/cueSettings/keepTimecodes/timeFormat to the Config struct (and implement them in the writer) or remove them from spec.config — currently 4 of 8 declared keys would be rejected by DisallowUnknownFields (D4, high).

Add a malformed_test asserting NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel for broken/truncated VTT (missing WEBVTT header, timecode-only, garbage bytes) and confirming Open rejects nil doc (D6, high).

Make the writer honor (or remove) the 4 real config keys (MaxCharsPerLine/MaxLinesPerCaption/CJKCharsPerLine/SplitWords) — they are read into Config but never consumed, so the schema/spec advertise behavior the writer does not implement (D3/D4, high).
formats.top_risk: spec.yaml/config drift: spec.config declares 8 keys but ApplyMap (ApplyMapViaJSON + DisallowUnknownFields) accepts only 4. Supplying discardCues/cueSettings/keepTimecodes/timeFormat would error 'unknown field'. The spec_test only passes because no example sets config — the contract is silently false.

blocking_gaps: HIGH (Malformed/robustness): add malformed_test.go feeding broken/truncated/non-UTF8 wiki input that asserts require.NotPanics AND a clean PartResult.Error surfaces on the channel — currently the reader's error-on-channel paths (reader.go:1044,1084) are never test-exercised. This is the single L2 dimension item keeping it below a clean L3.

MEDIUM (spec/config integrity): the spec.yaml config key is snake_case preserve_whitespace with okapi_param: preserve_whitespace, but the native Config struct json tag is camelCase preserveWhitespace and uses ApplyMapViaJSON+DisallowUnknownFields with NO snake_case alias / UnmarshalJSON. The spec comment claims 'the native reader accepts both spellings' but config.go has no such alias — so keys are not truly 1:1 with native ApplyMap. Add a snake_case alias (or UnmarshalJSON) and a config-applying example to make the claim true.

MEDIUM (L4 byte-faithful corpus): TestRoundTrip_WikiFiles and TestRoundTrip_DokuWikiTxtFiles only assert require.NotEmpty(output) — they do NOT assert byte/semantic equality over the full upstream dokuwiki.txt. Add a skeleton-store byte-exact (or documented-normalization) round-trip assertion over the real corpus and an edge-case matrix (CRLF, BOM, unicode).
formats.top_risk: spec/native config-key drift: spec key preserve_whitespace (snake_case, matching okapi_param) is silently never applied to the native reader — the parity runner only calls ApplyMap when an example sets config (none do), so the mismatch is masked. If any future example sets preserve_whitespace, runNative → ApplyMapViaJSON+DisallowUnknownFields would reject it as an unknown field, breaking parity. The spec's 'native accepts both spellings' comment is currently false (no alias in config.go).

blocking_gaps: L4: add an edge-case round-trip matrix asserting BYTE fidelity (not just re-parse) on CHANGED non-ASCII/unicode values — encode.go emits literal UTF-8 and never \uXXXX, so a changed value in a \uXXXX-style catalog (xckit fixture) would normalize escaping; this divergence is currently untested for byte-equality (invariants only mutate with ASCII 'TR:'/'T:' prefixes and re-parse semantically)
L4: add edge-case coverage for line endings (CRLF) and BOM — current corpus/fixtures are all LF; the retokenize-splice should preserve them but it is not asserted
L4: add schema_test asserting Config.Schema() property set == ApplyMap key set (schema==config), as html/json have
formats.top_risk: Encoding normalization on CHANGED values: rewrite.go emitReplacedString re-encodes via encode.go encodeJSONString, which emits non-ASCII as literal UTF-8 and never \uXXXX. For a catalog authored with \uXXXX escapes (the real xckit corpus file), translating a non-ASCII value would silently change the escaping style of that one value. Byte-faithfulness is only proven for UNCHANGED catalogs (locale="") and for ASCII-only mutations; the non-ASCII-changed-value path has no byte-equality assertion.

blocking_gaps: Add a malformed_test.go: feed truncated XLIFF, unclosed tags, garbage bytes, and a non-well-formed prolog; assert require.NotPanics around reader.Read and that a clean PartResult.Error lands on the channel (currently only nil-doc is covered by TestReadNilDocument). This is the gating L2 robustness item.

Add a schema_test.go that asserts schema.go Properties/field set stays in sync with config.go ApplyMap keys (schema==config), the L4 anti-drift guard the rubric calls out (only html/json have it today).
formats.top_risk: No malformed/truncated-input test: the reader's tolerant transcoding (windows-1252 fallback, C0-control sanitization to U+FFFD, Strict=false decoder) is well-engineered but UNTESTED against deliberately broken input — a panic or a swallowed-error path could regress unnoticed. ABSENCE of malformed_test is a real robustness gap.

blocking_gaps: Writer (critical): TestRoundTrip_ByteEqualUntouched is RED on 22 real upstream fixtures (simple.xlf, notes.xlf, update_target.xlf, test02.xlf, test_unit_37.xlf2, subfilter_html/icu/json) — either fix the DOM round-trip to pass these through byte-exact (XML decl encoding injection at writer.go:267, segment-id synthesis at :605, stripOkapiDefaults/propagateXMLSpace re-serialization) or expand notExpectedByteEqual() with attributed justifications (currently only 3 of 25 are documented).
Add a malformed_test (L2 gate): broken/truncated/non-XML input must yield a clean PartResult.Error on the channel under assert.NotPanics; the parsing-error path (reader.go:130) is currently untested.

Add explicit invariants/acceptance assertions distinguishing reader normalization (data loss) from cosmetic re-serialization for the 22 failing fixtures, so the byte-equal contract states what it actually guarantees.
formats.top_risk: TestRoundTrip_ByteEqualUntouched FAILS on 22 upstream fixtures while only 3 are in the documented notExpectedByteEqual() allow-list. The package test currently FAILS (go test ./core/formats/xliff2 -tags fts5 -> FAIL). This is the headline correctness risk: the writer's 'byte-equal on untouched round-trip' contract is broken on real-world inputs and the failures are largely unattributed.

blocking_gaps: Add malformed_test.go: feed truncated/unclosed/broken XML and a nil doc; assert NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error on the channel and Open(nil) error — this is the L3-blocking high-weight gap (dim 6 currently 'none').

Make Config.ApplyMap reject unknown keys (replace the 'silently ignore for forward compat' default branches at config.go:504-505 and parser:500-501 with an error) — satisfies the L1/Config 'rejects unknown keys' contract; or document+justify the forward-compat choice if intentional.
Add a schema_test asserting schema.go property set == ApplyMap key set (prevents schema/config drift).
formats.top_risk: ApplyMap silently swallows unknown/misspelled config keys (config.go:504-505) and unknown parser sub-keys (config.go:500-501): a typo'd rule (e.g. 'tranlatableElements') is a no-op with no error, so a misconfigured project extracts wrong content silently. The bridge_config side, by contrast, errors on unknown keys (xmlstream_bridge_config.go:170) — asymmetric strictness between native and parity paths.

blocking_gaps: Add core/formats/yaml/malformed_test.go: feed truncated/broken YAML (bad indentation, unterminated quote, invalid tag, garbage bytes) and assert require.NotPanics + a clean PartResult.Error arrives on the channel (NOT returned from Open). This is the single required L2 item that is missing and the only thing keeping the format at L1 — everything else for L2 (spec.yaml + green spec_test, schema.go, config rejects unknowns) is already satisfied.
Add an invariants/acceptance assertion that the parse-error path is exercised end-to-end (Open succeeds, Read yields an Error part) so robustness is locked, not just panic-free.
formats.top_risk: No malformed-input test at all: yaml.v3 Decode errors are forwarded to the channel (reader.go:155,170) but there is no test proving NotPanics or that a clean Error actually surfaces for broken/truncated input — robustness is unverified.
